data.description:
RNS Number : 0361A GoldmanSachs International 10 March 2025  

FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY STATUS DEALING IN A CLIENT-SERVING CAPACITY

Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

1.         KEY INFORMATION

(a) Name of exempt principal trader:

(GS) GOLDMAN SACHS INTERNATIONAL

(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:

     Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree

ENQUEST PLC

(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:

ENQUEST PLC

(d) Date dealing undertaken:

07 March 2025

(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?

     If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"

YES - disclosing today for SERICA ENERGY PLC

2.         DEALINGS BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

(ii)        Exercise

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

    e.g. subscription, conversion

3.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

Date of disclosure:

10 March 2025

Contact name:

Papa Lette and Andrzej Szyszka

Telephone number:

+33(1) 4212 1459 / +48(22) 317 4817

data.title: REG - Baillie Gifford UK - Net Asset Value(s)
data.description:
RNS Number : 0364A Baillie Gifford UK Growth Trust PLC 10 March 2025  

Baillie Gifford UK Growth Trust plc (BGUK)

07 March 2025

Legal Entity Identifier : 549300XX386SYWX8XW22

Cum Par NAV

204.15p

Ex Par NAV

199.81p

Short-term borrowings are valued at par.

Net Asset Values are unaudited, and calculated in accordance with published accounting policies and AIC guidelines.

Cum Par NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, including income, with debt at par value.

Ex Par NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, excluding income, with debt at par value.

data.title: Raymond James Downgrades Occidental Petroleum to Outperform From Strong Buy, Price Target is $64
data.description: Occidental Petroleum has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $60.46, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.description:

data.title: South Korean singer Wheesung found dead at home - Yonhap
data.description:
South Korean singer Wheesung has been found dead, Yonhap News reported citing police on Monday.

The 43-year-old's body was discovered at his home in Seoul, less than a month after actress Kim Sae-ron was found dead after her career had taken a hit following a drunk driving incident in 2022.

Police were not immediately available for comment.

Net Asset Values are unaudited, and calculated in accordance with published accounting policies and AIC guidelines.

Cum Par NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, including income, with debt at par value.

Cum Fair NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, including income, with debt at fair value.

Ex Par NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, excluding income, with debt at par value.

Ex Fair NAV: Net asset value per share in pence, excluding income, with debt at fair value.

data.title: B. Riley Trims Price Target on Clarus to $5 From $5.50, Keeps Buy Rating
data.description: Clarus has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $5.21, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Athabasca Oil Upgraded to Outperform at RBC
data.description:
Athabasca Oil Corp. was upgraded to Outperform from Sector Perform at RBC Capital Markets on Monday.

Analyst Greg Pardy raised his price target on shares of the Calgary-based energy company to $6.50 from $6 following recent meetings with senior management.

"In our minds, Athabasca Oil possesses everything we are looking for in an energy producer - a capable leadership team, shareholder alignment, solid operating performance, a strong balance sheet, organic growth profile and 100% payout of (thermal) free cash flow to shareholders," Pardy said in a note to clients.

"Given its recent pullback, we have upgraded Athabasca to Outperform (given our confidence in its outlook)," the analyst said.

data.title: Tariff Concerns, Ukraine-Russia War Outlook Dampen European Bourses Midday
data.description:
European bourses tracked moderately lower midday Monday as traders awaited clarity on tariffs from President Donald Trump and for clues to the outlook of the Ukraine-Russian war.

Real estate, food and oil stocks bucked trends to gain, while bank and tech issues lagged.

Investors also eyed Wall Street futures signaling red, and uneven closes overnight on Asian exchanges. The Hong Kong Hang Seng index fell back 1.9% after soft inflation reports from Beijing renewed concerns the huge China economy is stalling.

In economic news, the Eurozone Sentix Investor Confidence indicator logged at negative 2.9 in March, compared with negative 12.7 in February.

In other news, HSBC on Monday downgraded US equities on tariff turmoil, but turned bullish on European stocks, citing likely heavier fiscal outlays in Germany.

The pan-continental Stoxx Europe 600 Index was off 0.6% mid-session.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Technology Index was off 1.6%, and the Stoxx 600 Banks Index lost 1.4%.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Oil and Gas Index was up 0.2%, while the Stoxx 600 Europe Food and Beverage Index edged 0.3% higher.

The REITE, a European REIT index, rose 1.2%, while the Stoxx Europe 600 Retail Index declined 0.2%.

On the national market indexes, Germany's DAX was down 1%, and the FTSE 100 in London was 0.4% lower. The CAC 40 in Paris was off 0.5%, and Spain's IBEX 35 lost 1%.

Yields on benchmark 10-year German bonds were steady, near 2.8%.

Front-month North Sea Brent crude-oil futures were up 0.4% at $70.66 per barrel.

The Euro Stoxx 50 volatility index was up 1.7% at 23.04, indicating above-average volatility for European stock markets in the next 30 days, a negative signal. A reading above 20 indicates choppier markets ahead, while below 20 suggests calmer exchanges.

data.title: Fastenal Raised to Outperform From Market Perform by William Blair
ChromaDex Is Maintained at Buy by HC Wainwright & Co.
data.description: (11:50 GMT) ChromaDex Price Target Raised to $11.00/Share From $8.00 by HC Wainwright & Co.

data.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Palo Alto Networks Price Target to $207 From $220, Maintains Outperform Rating
data.description: Palo Alto Networks has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $216.07, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Market Chatter: CVS to Open Smaller, Pharmacy-Focused Stores, WSJ Reports
data.description:
CVS Health is preparing to launch 12 smaller stores focused primarily on pharmacy services, with minimal space for general retail, the Wall Street Journal reported Saturday, citing a company spokesperson.

The outlets, each under 5,000 square feet, will be less than half the size of a normal CVS and are scheduled to open during 2026 in multiple locations, the WSJ added.

The new stores will reportedly stock core health essentials only and omit general merchandise.

CVS did not immediately respond to MT Newswires request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: B. Riley Cuts Price Target on TripAdvisor to $18 From $20, Keeps Neutral Rating
data.description: TripAdvisor has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $18.13, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Polarean Imaging's New Xenon MRI Selected for Lung Therapy Trial
data.description:
Polarean Imaging said an unnamed global pharmaceutical partner enlisted its new Xenon Magnetic Resonance Imaging, or MRI, clinical trial support services platform.

The platform was selected as part of a sub-study within a worldwide study trial for an investigational lung therapy, according to a Monday filing from the medical imaging technology company.

Polarean introduced the new Xenon MRI service model in partnership with Vida Diagnostics.

data.title: Tai Hing Group Holdings Expects Lower Attributable Profit for 2024
data.description:
Tai Hing Group Holdings expects a profit attributable to the owners between HK$60 million and HK$65 million for 2024, lower than HK$93.8 million from 2023, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The restaurant group attributed the expected decrease to northbound consumption inclination of Hong Kong residents, a downgrade in consumption in Hong Kong and China resulting from the economic conditions, and the impairment of provisions for assets and closure-related expenses during the reporting period.

data.title: Market Chatter: Full Truck Alliance May Revisit Plan for Second Listing in Hong Kong
data.description:
Full Truck Alliance will "actively re-examine" and consider a listing in Hong Kong amid a rebound in investor interest and growing geopolitical tensions between China and the US, Reuters reported Monday, citing Chief Financial Officer Simon Cai.

The Chinese company had initially planned a dual primary listing in Hong Kong in 2022 due to stricter audit requirements for US-listed Chinese companies, according to the report. It went public in New York in 2021, Reuters reported.

The company's shares were down 1.9% in recent premarket activity.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: IBM wins UK lawsuit against LzLabs over alleged theft of mainframe technology
data.description:
IBM on Monday won a London lawsuit against U.S. tech entrepreneur and philanthropist John Moores' company LzLabs, which the IT giant accused of stealing trade secrets.

IBM sued Switzerland-based LzLabs, two English subsidiaries and their ultimate owner Moores, who previously owned Major League Baseball's San Diego Padres team and founded information technology company BMC Software in 1980, at the High Court.

The company alleged LzLabs' UK subsidiary Winsopia purchased an IBM mainframe computer and agreed a licence with IBM in 2013, before using the access to reverse-engineer IBM's mainframe software.

LzLabs and Moores fought the lawsuit, arguing LzLabs developed its own software after nearly a decade of work and there was no unlawful use of IBM's licensed software.

But the High Court largely ruled in IBM's favour, with Judge Finola O'Farrell saying in a written ruling that Winsopia breached the terms of its IBM software licence and that "LzLabs and Mr Moores unlawfully procured (those) breaches."

Monday's ruling followed a trial last year to determine whether the defendants were liable to IBM, with a further hearing to determine any damages to take place in due course.

IBM's case against another British subsidiary, LzLabs Limited, and LzLabs' current and former CEO was rejected.

IBM and LzLabs did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Moores could not immediately be reached for comment.

data.title: Genie Energy Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Genie Energy, Ltd., a leading retail energy and renewable energy solutions provider, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company reported a solid performance across its retail and renewables businesses, achieving the high end of its Adjusted EBITDA guidance for the year.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Genie Energy reported a slight decrease in revenue by 1.9% to $102.9 million compared to $104.9 million in the same period last year. Gross profit also saw a minor decline of 0.5% to $33.5 million, although the gross margin increased to 32.5% from 32.1%. The company narrowed its loss from operations to $20.8 million, down from $34.2 million in the fourth quarter of 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter decreased by 2.8% to $11.1 million.

For the full year 2024, revenue slightly decreased by 0.8% to $425.2 million from $428.7 million in 2023. Gross profit fell by 5.3% to $138.5 million, with a gross margin of 32.6%. Income from operations increased to $11.3 million from $10.0 million in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $48.5 million, down from $58.2 million in 2023. Net income attributable to Genie common stockholders was $12.3 million, or $0.46 per diluted share, compared to $20.3 million, or $0.74 per diluted share, in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Genie Retail Energy (GRE) added over 60,000 net meters in 2024, expanding its customer base by 17%. The fourth quarter alone saw an addition of over 23,000 net meters. Despite a slight decrease in revenue, GRE's income from operations and Adjusted EBITDA were impacted by reduced electricity margins and increased customer acquisition expenses.

Genie Renewables (GREW) experienced a 30.1% decrease in fourth-quarter revenue to $4.5 million, primarily due to Genie Solar's exit from the commercial-scale solar project development market. However, the full-year revenue for GREW increased by 16.1% to $21.9 million, driven by growth at Diversegy, the company's energy procurement business. The loss from operations for GREW narrowed significantly, reflecting improved performance at Diversegy and reduced operating expenses at Genie Solar.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Genie Energy continued to advance its strategic initiatives, including the completion of its migration to the utility-scale project vertical at Genie Solar. The company also closed a financing deal for its portfolio of currently operating arrays. Additionally, Genie Energy repurchased approximately 661,000 shares for $10.4 million in 2024 and paid a $0.075 per share quarterly dividend to Class A and Class B common stockholders.

Management's Perspective

CEO Michael Stein commented on the company's performance, stating, "Genie finished 2024 with a solid fourth quarter across both our retail and renewables businesses, even as we continued to invest significantly in growth initiatives. For the full year, we achieved the high end of our Adjusted EBITDA guidance."

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to 2025, Genie Energy maintains its annual consolidated Adjusted EBITDA guidance at $40 to $50 million. The company also expects to continue building its cash reserves and opportunistically buy back stock while maintaining its current dividend.

SEC Filing: Genie Energy Ltd. [ GNE ] - 8-K - Mar. 10, 2025
